title: How to Build a Vanity Mirror
summary: A framed bathroom vanity mirror with a solid wood surround and an optional little shelf — dresses up a plain 24x36 mirror for the cost of a few 1-by boards.
description:
A builder-grade frameless mirror looks unfinished; wrap it in a wood frame and it becomes a feature. This build frames a standard 24" x 36" mirror with a 1x8 and 1x6 header and footer, 1x3 sides, and a 1x2 border, giving a substantial surround you can paint or stain to match the room. An optional shelf with angled brackets turns it into a place for a plant or a jar of brushes.

The frame is assembled face-down and flat: lay the boards out, arrange for square, and join them — pocket holes make the tidiest joints, but the long 1x2 border laid over the top and screwed and glued with 1-1/4" fasteners also locks everything together. Check square with diagonal measurements before the glue sets; a mirror frame that's even slightly out of square reads as crooked on the wall.

The mirror mounts to the back of the completed frame with mirror clips (glue is a belt-and-suspenders addition). The single most important thing here is hanging: a 24x36 mirror is heavy and it's over a sink, so use a heavy-duty picture-hanging kit into a stud or a properly rated drywall anchor. This is not a place to trust a single small hook.

tools.note: Tidiest frame joints; screws and glue also work.

materials.name: 1x4 board, 6 ft
materials.note: Optional shelf and its angled brackets.

materials.name: Heavy-duty picture-hanging kit
materials.note: Rated for a heavy mirror over a sink.

cutList.note: Cut out at an angle as brackets.

steps.title: Assemble the frame
steps.body: Lay the 1x3 sides, 1x6 footer, and 1x8 header down and arrange for square. Join them — pocket screws into the header and footer are cleanest. Lay the long 1x2 border on top around the outside and screw and glue with 1-1/4" fasteners. Check diagonals and adjust for square before the glue grabs.

steps.title: Add the optional shelf
steps.body: If you want a shelf, cut the two supports into angled brackets, fasten them under the shelf board, and attach the assembly to the frame. Skip this step for a plain mirror.

steps.title: Finish the frame
steps.body: Sand the frame smooth and paint or stain it to match the bathroom before the mirror goes in, so you don't get finish on the glass.

steps.title: Mount the mirror and hang
steps.body: Set the mirror into the back of the frame and secure it with mirror clips (a bead of mirror-safe adhesive is optional insurance). Attach a heavy-duty picture-hanging kit, then hang into a stud or a properly rated drywall anchor and level it. Never trust a single light hook with a mirror over a sink.

images.alt: Wood-framed bathroom vanity mirror with an optional shelf
